interviewStages: Behavioral: customer focus, navigating ambiguity, working with PMs and design
codingTopics: OO design: model a business domain such as workflows with triggers and actions
Multi-tenant data structures: per-tenant rate limiting, isolation
Streaming: rolling aggregates over tenant events
Graph and DAG: workflow evaluation, dependency resolution
Search and filters: AND/OR queries over records
systemDesignTopics: Design an event ingestion pipeline that handles 1B events/day
Design a JavaScript SDK that stays under 60KB gzipped and does not slow customer apps
Design an in-app guide editor that picks DOM elements without breaking on customer DOM changes

coding: At Pendo, model a workflow engine with triggers, conditions, and actions. Evaluate a workflow DAG and detect cycles.
Implement a per-tenant rate limiter so one customer cannot exhaust shared capacity. Discuss the token-bucket design.
Given a stream of tenant events, maintain rolling per-entity scores with bounded memory.
Implement a filter query over records supporting AND/OR on custom fields. Discuss indexing for a multi-tenant store.
Deduplicate records via fuzzy clustering on name and email. Discuss the matching and complexity.
behavioral: Tell me about a project you are proud of at Pendo scale and the hardest tradeoff you made.
Give me an example that shows customer focus, navigating ambiguity, working with pms and design.
Describe a time you disagreed with a teammate or manager. How did it resolve?
Tell me about a time you took ownership of an ambiguous problem end to end.
systemDesign: Design an event ingestion pipeline that handles 1B events/day.
Design a JavaScript SDK that stays under 60KB gzipped and does not slow customer apps.
Design an in-app guide editor that picks DOM elements without breaking on customer DOM changes.
values: Pendo succeeds when its customers do. Describe a time you solved for the customer even at a cost to the team.
Tell me about a time you handled a tricky multi-tenant isolation or permissions problem.
How do you balance enterprise customization against product simplicity?
other: How does Pendo compare to Amplitude or Mixpanel?

rawSummary:
Pendo Interview Guide (2026): Product Analytics and In-App Guides
By 			
				
				techinterview.org			
			
		

		 /  May 5, 2026 
			
		

	
	
	

		
		
On this page

- Process

- What they actually ask

- Levels and comp (2026)

- Prep priorities

- Frequently Asked Questions

Updated May 5, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Pendo
pendo.io ↗

Pendo combines product analytics with in-app guidance — the unique pitch is "see what users do AND nudge them via tooltips and walkthroughs without code." Used by 8000+ companies. The interview is data-engineering and frontend-instrumentation heavy, with focus on the unique constraints of running a JavaScript SDK inside customer apps.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding phone (DSA medium) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Cycle: 3–4 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design an event ingestion pipeline that handles 1B events/day

- Design a JavaScript SDK that stays under 60KB gzipped and does not slow customer apps

- Design an in-app guide editor that picks DOM elements without breaking on customer DOM changes

- Coding: medium DSA with browser/DOM flavor for frontend roles

- Behavioral: customer focus, navigating ambiguity, working with PMs and design

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $145K–$180K total

- Senior SE: $210K–$280K

- Staff: $300K–$400K

- Principal: $420K–$550K

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in TypeScript/JavaScript for SDK; Java for backend

- Understand columnar storage and analytical query optimization

- Know browser internals (DOM, MutationObserver, performance)

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Pendo remote-friendly?

Hybrid in Raleigh (HQ), San Francisco, Tel Aviv, and others. Many engineering roles are remote within US.

How does Pendo compare to Amplitude or Mixpanel?

Pendo combines analytics with guidance, a unique combo. Smaller than Amplitude on pure analytics; bigger than competing in-app guidance vendors. Comp lags Amplitude by ~10–15%.

What is the engineering culture?

Mid-stage SaaS — pragmatic, customer-driven, decent work-life balance. Not a tech-darling brand but solid product engineering work.
